2.title: Italian Leonardo and Turkish Baykar jointly test unmanned aerial vehicles
2.content: The Italian defense company Leonardo and the Turkish aerospace company Baykar have successfully concluded the first joint test flights of their new unmanned aerial systems, marking a significant milestone in bilateral defense cooperation.

4.title: The Mil Mi-12: This giant helicopter designed by the USSR to push the limits of aviation
4.content: Only two prototypes of this helicopter from Russian manufacturer Mil were ever built. Its unprecedented dimensions completely disrupted the world of aviation. The Mi-12 entered service in 1968 and was retired in 1974.

7.title: 2026 World Cup bombshell: Norway pulls off a stunning upset against Brazil
7.content: A massive shockwave hit the 2026 World Cup with the surprise elimination of Brazil in the round of 16, defeated 2-1 by Norway on Sunday night in New Jersey. Erling Haaland proved to be Brazil's executioner, netting a brilliant and memorable brace in the 79th and 90th minutes, alongside a series of decisive, brilliant saves from Norwegian goalkeeper Nyland.

5.title: Kylian Mbappé transformed at the World Cup as France continues to shine
5.content: Criticized throughout the season, Kylian Mbappé appears completely transformed since the start of the World Cup, prompting many fans to change their minds about him. The French team has been on absolute fire since the tournament began. After winning all three of their group stage matches against Senegal, Iraq, and Norway, Les Bleus made a strong impression once again on Monday during their round of 16 clash against Sweden.

8.title: Cristiano Ronaldo facing heavy backlash from Portuguese media
8.content: Several Portuguese media headlines have been harsh on Cristiano Ronaldo following the Seleção's round of 16 elimination against Spain on Monday. The 0-1 defeat in Dallas was sealed by a 90th-minute goal from Mikel Merino.

9.title: A staggering €223 million transfer for Michael Olise?
9.content: Real Madrid is reportedly targeting Michael Olise, with Los Merengues ready to spend a record-breaking €223 million to secure the services of the Bayern Munich winger.

10.title: The World Cup Robbery: Social media erupts over alleged FIFA rigging to save Messi
10.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup match between Egypt and Argentina ended in a massive refereeing scandal following Argentina's controversial 3-2 victory. Fans are calling it a disgrace, claiming FIFA historically protects Argentina and rigged the match.
